name: 2023-03-asymmetry
note: Asymmetry safETH liquid staking (SafEth.sol + Reth/SfrxEth/WstEth derivatives). In scope: contracts/SafEth (4 contracts). A conservative, high-confidence subset of the official C4 report (8 High / 12 Medium), each pinned to a verified source line. IDs carry the report's H-/M- number.

known_findings.summary: stake() derives preDepositPrice = 10**18 * underlyingValue / totalSupply from live derivative balances; an early/sole staker can unstake down to a tiny supply and donate derivative tokens to inflate underlyingValue, so the next depositor's mintAmount rounds to near-zero shares (first-depositor / share-price inflation, H-01).

unstake() loops every derivative and calls derivatives[i].withdraw(); there is no way to remove a broken/untrusted derivative, so if one withdraw reverts (e.g. WstEth's Curve pool killed) the whole unstake reverts and all users' ETH is locked (H-03). Protocol-specific DoS-on-revert, no modeled detector.

SfrxEth.ethPerDerivative returns (10**18 * frxAmount) / price_oracle() — the price_oracle multiplication is inverted (should be frxAmount * price_oracle / 10**18), so the sfrxETH derivative is valued incorrectly, mispricing stake/unstake (H-04). Protocol-specific price-formula bug, no modeled detector.

Reth.poolPrice computes (sqrtPriceX96 * sqrtPriceX96 * 1e18) >> 192 with no overflow guard (unlike Uniswap's OracleLibrary); for large sqrtPriceX96 the multiplication overflows and reverts under 0.8.x checked math, bricking the price path and thus stake/unstake (H-05).

WstEth.withdraw computes minOut = stEthBal * (1e18 - maxSlippage) / 1e18, implicitly assuming 1 stETH = 1 ETH; during a stETH de-peg the Curve exchange returns less than minOut and reverts, blocking withdrawals (H-06). Protocol-specific peg assumption, no modeled detector.
Reth.deposit performs a Uniswap V3 swapExactInputSingleHop with no deadline parameter, so a pending stake transaction can be held by validators and executed later at an unfavorable rate (M-04).

unstake() sends the withdrawn ETH with a single address(msg.sender).call{value:...} and requires success; a contract caller whose receive reverts (or a push-payment failure) blocks the withdrawal even though every derivative withdraw succeeded (M-06). Protocol-specific DoS-on-revert (precise sub-label push-payment-dos, modeled by the dos.rs Pattern-4 push-payment arm); stays in_class:false.

unstake() loops over all derivatives (for i < derivativeCount), each doing external balance/withdraw calls; as derivativeCount grows the loop can exceed the block gas limit and make unstake permanently revert (M-08).

stake() accepts no user-supplied minimum-output (min safETH) parameter; the per-derivative deposits swap on AMMs and the final mintAmount has no slippage bound, so a user can be sandwiched and minted fewer shares than expected (M-12).
